old_claim.note: Orbital Manufacturing is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: Mining provides the excavation and material-extraction capability needed to collect lunar regolith, ice, and other resources before processing.

source_shape.source_locator: ISRU Investments and Regolith-Based Volatiles Acquisition and Processing Technology: NASA describes excavating or drilling into regolith-based water deposits and processing, transporting, and storing resources as oxygen, water, and methane.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA identifies excavation or drilling into regolith-based water deposits, followed by processing and storage of resources, as a core ISRU investment area.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This makes extraction and material handling the direct prerequisite family for lunar resource utilization, while orbital manufacturing is an off-surface production context that may consume ISRU products later.

ontology_before: The graph made broad lunar extraction and processing depend on orbital manufacturing, implying an orbital production capability was needed before lunar surface resource use.
ontology_after: The graph routes lunar resource utilization through mining, keeping the node anchored to extraction of lunar regolith, ice, and materials before downstream manufacturing or export applications.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: lunar_resource_utilization now depends on mining and no longer directly on orbital_manufacturing.
would_reject_if: The lunar resource node is later rescoped to orbital factories using lunar feedstock rather than extraction and processing on or from the lunar surface.
A source shows orbital manufacturing is a direct prerequisite for lunar regolith, ice, oxygen, or metal extraction.
short_reason: Mining is the direct extraction capability; orbital manufacturing is a downstream or adjacent production context.
